defs: A plate forming an exact facsimile of a page of type or of an engraving, used in printing books, etc.; specifically, a plate with type-metal face, used for printing.
notes: ☞ A stereotype, or stereotypr plate, is made by setting movable type as for ordinary printing; from these a cast is taken in plaster of Paris, paper pulp, or the like, and upon this cast melted type metal is poured, which, when hardened, makes a solid page or column, from which the impression is taken as from type.

defs: The art or process of making such plates, or of executing work by means of them.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Stereotyped (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Stereotyping (?).]

defs: To prepare for printing in stereotype; to make the stereotype plates of; as, to stereotype the Bible.
Fig.: To make firm or permanent; to fix.
quotes: Powerful causes tending to stereotype and aggravate the poverty of old conditions.
